raw_transcript_text:
DOCTOR: Namaste, kya problem hai? Aaj aap kaise aaye?

PATIENT: Namaste doctor sahab. Mera sugar ka number pichle kuch mahine se upar chal raha hai. Thoda weak bhi feel karta hoon.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, dekhte hain. Pehle batao—morning mein kya aata hai? Fasting ka number?

PATIENT: Haan, fasting toh 180-200 ke beech mein aata hai. Aur khaane ke baad do ghante mein 260 tak chala jaata hai.

DOCTOR: Hmm, theek nahi hai. Kya dawai chal rahi hai abhi?

PATIENT: Haan, Glycomet 500 le raha hoon subah aur raat, lekin lagta hai kaam nahi kar raha.

DOCTOR: Kitne saal se diabetes hai? Aur koi family mein hai?

PATIENT: Pichle 6-7 saal se hai. Mere papa ko bhi tha, aur ek bhai ko bhi.

DOCTOR: Samajh gaya. Aaj weight kitna hai? Height batao.

PATIENT: Weight 78 kg hai, height 5 foot 6.

DOCTOR: Theek hai. BP check karte hain pehle. Aaram se baitho, haath dheela rakho. — BP 145/90 hai, thoda high hai. Pulse 84 hai. Temperature normal hai. SpO2 98 hai, sahi hai.

PATIENT: Koi problem to nahi hai doctor?

DOCTOR: Koi baat nahi, hum sambhal lenge. Aankhon mein dhundaapan ya paani aana? Haath-pair mein jhunjhunaahat?

PATIENT: Haan, kabhi kabhi pair mein jhunjhunaahat hoti hai aur raat ko baarish jaisa feel hota hai.

DOCTOR: Yeh peripheral neuropathy ke signs hain. Theek hai, mai ek baar pet bhi dekhta hoon. — Abdomen soft hai, koi mass nahi. Chalo, ab prescription likhte hain.

DIAGNOSIS: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with hypertension (Stage 1) and early peripheral neuropathy.

PRESCRIPTION:

1. Glycomet 500 mg — One tablet after breakfast, one after dinner (continue)
2. Telma-40 mg — One tablet in the morning for BP
3. Shelcal 500 mg — One tablet in the morning for calcium & vit D
4. Pan-D 40 mg — One capsule empty stomach in the morning for acidity (if needed)
5. For neuropathy — Methylcobalamin 500 mcg one tablet three times a day for 1 month

Duration: 1 month. Then follow-up with HbA1c, kidney function test, urine routine.

PATIENT: Theek hai doctor. Aur koi pabandi hai khane mein?

DOCTOR: Haan, chawal aur aloo limit karo. Roti, sabzi, salad aur dal accha rahega. Toh aaj se dawai shuru karo aur ek mahine mein report leke aao. Koi baat nahi, control ho jayega.

PATIENT: Dhanyavaad doctor.

soap_note.subjective: Patient reports that his blood sugar levels have been elevated for the past few months, with fasting blood glucose between 180-200 mg/dL and postprandial (2 hours after meals) up to 260 mg/dL. He also feels weak. He has been taking Glycomet 500 mg twice daily (after breakfast and dinner) but feels it is not working. History of diabetes for 6-7 years; family history positive (father and brother). He reports occasional tingling sensation in feet and feeling like 'urination' at night, concerning for peripheral neuropathy. No complaints of blurred vision or excessive urination.
soap_note.objective: Weight: 78 kg, Height: 5'6" (168 cm), BP: 145/90 mmHg (elevated), Pulse: 84/min, Temperature: normal, SpO2: 98% (normal). Abdomen: soft, no masses. Clinical signs of early peripheral neuropathy present.
soap_note.assessment: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with poor glycemic control, hypertension (Stage 1), and early peripheral neuropathy.
soap_note.plan: Continue Glycomet 500 mg (one tablet after breakfast, one after dinner). Add Telma-40 mg (telmisartan) one tablet in the morning for blood pressure. Add Shelcal 500 mg (calcium + vitamin D) one tablet in the morning. Add Pan-D 40 mg (pantoprazole) one capsule empty stomach in the morning if needed for acidity. For neuropathy: Methylcobalamin 500 mcg one tablet three times daily for 1 month. Dietary advice: limit rice and potatoes, increase roti, vegetables, salad, and dal. Follow up in 1 month with HbA1c, kidney function test, and urinalysis. [VERIFY] if Pan-D is intended for routine use or as needed.
